text.title: FinOps — Spot / Preemptible Instances for Fault-Tolerant Workloads Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the fco-spot-instances methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale and is referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: Spot workloads MUST checkpoint state at interval ≤ 5 minutes; uncheckpointed compute on spot is forbidden because interruption forfeits all in-progress work.

rationale.source: AWS Spot docs, GCP preemptible docs

rule.rationale: Without checkpoints, interruption resets the job; the savings are erased by re-execution cost.

rule.statement: Workload MUST implement an interruption-notice handler (Instance Metadata Service / preemption hook) that drains gracefully within 90 seconds.

rationale.source: AWS Spot Instance interruption notice docs

rule.rationale: Ungraceful interruption produces partial writes and orphaned tasks; the 2-minute warning is the only chance to drain.

rule.statement: Spot fleet MUST diversify across ≥ 3 instance types AND ≥ 2 AZs; single-instance-family spot is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Single-family spot pools spike to zero capacity during demand surges; diversification keeps the fleet alive.

rule.statement: Max spot price MUST be capped at on-demand price; bidding above on-demand defeats the entire saving rationale.
rule.rationale: Above-on-demand bids exist for rare cases; default policy is never to exceed on-demand.

rule.statement: Critical-path workload MUST keep a minimum on-demand fleet (≥ 10% of capacity) so a complete spot interruption does not zero the service.

rationale.source: AWS EC2 Fleet docs, GCP preemptible best practices

rule.rationale: Total fleet zero is a real risk during spot reclamation events; the on-demand floor prevents full outage.

rule.statement: If the workload is stateful without checkpoint capability OR latency-critical with no replacement gap tolerance, this methodology MUST be skipped.
rule.rationale: Spot under those constraints produces an outage, not savings.
